test.details: 1354 tests passed across 33 test files, 0 failures

lint.details: No lint errors in production code. Warnings are pre-existing in test files (unused variables/constants from kernel module includes), not from mission features.

allIssues.description: glyph_id u32→u16 truncation in shaping library (info.glyph_id as u16) lacks debug_assert. Safe per OpenType spec but a debug_assert would catch corrupt font data.
build.rs rerun-if-changed doesn't watch shaping/Cargo.lock — dependency updates without Cargo.toml changes won't re-trigger build.
truetype.rs retains dead type definitions (GlyphMetrics, GlyphOutline, etc.) with #[allow(dead_code)] — these are duplicated in shaping/src/rasterize.rs. Technical debt to clean up.
Nested composite glyphs are skipped in extract_outline() — same limitation as old parser, not a regression.
drawing/lib.rs module doc says 'no allocations' but now uses extern crate alloc (BTreeMap, Vec in cache). Doc is stale.
LruGlyphCache has len() but no is_empty() — would trigger clippy lint.

Missing // SAFETY: comments on unsafe blocks in DoubleWriter::front_shaped_glyphs and DoubleReader::front_shaped_glyphs. Follows pre-existing pattern where all DoubleWriter/DoubleReader unsafe blocks lack SAFETY comments.

glyph_id u16→u8 truncation in scene_render.rs (cache.get(sg.glyph_id as u8)). Works for current monospace bridging but latent issue for proportional text. Will need fixing in unicode-and-fallback milestone.
prop_cache field in RenderCtx is populated but never used in rendering path. Unused code.
bytes_to_shaped_glyphs duplicated identically in core/scene_state.rs and compositor/scene_state.rs. Should be extracted to scene library.
Core service uses manual bytes_to_shaped_glyphs bridging instead of calling shaping::shape(). Documented as intentional for current monospace-only state.
Silent return on allocation failure in GlyphCache::populate() leaves cache empty with no diagnostic output.

appliedUpdates.description: Fixed incorrect API reference: info.as_glyph() → info.glyph_id (pub(crate) vs public field). Fixed Feature::from_str() → "+liga".parse::<Feature>() (correct FromStr trait usage).
Added RasterScratch (~39 KiB) to large-struct list alongside SvgPath and SvgRasterScratch. Documented Box::from_raw pattern (never Box::new for large structs).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'When a utility function is needed by two or more services, extract it to the appropriate shared library (e.g., scene library for scene-graph-related helpers). Do not duplicate code across services.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: bytes_to_shaped_glyphs is duplicated identically in core/scene_state.rs and compositor/scene_state.rs. The scene library already hosts layout helpers (layout_mono_lines, scroll_runs) — this function belongs there.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add SAFETY comment enforcement note: 'The DoubleWriter/DoubleReader types in scene/lib.rs have a pre-existing gap where unsafe blocks lack // SAFETY: comments. When touching these types, add SAFETY comments to new AND existing unsafe blocks.' Or schedule a bulk fix.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: scene/lib.rs: ~10 unsafe blocks in DoubleWriter/DoubleReader lack SAFETY comments (front_nodes, front_text_runs, front_shaped_glyphs in both types). New code follows the existing pattern rather than fixing it.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Font-rendering-worker skill could acknowledge TDD exceptions: 'Some bare-metal bugs (stack overflow, DMA alignment) cannot be reproduced in host-side tests due to environmental differences. For environment-specific bugs, QEMU visual verification is the primary validation — document why host-side TDD is not applicable.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-compositor-qemu-crash worker correctly identified that a 39 KiB stack overflow (16 KiB bare-metal stack) cannot be reproduced on host (8 MiB stack). Worker used QEMU verification instead. The skill mandates TDD without acknowledging this exception.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Font-rendering-worker skill could document the include!() module pattern: 'The drawing library uses include!("module.rs") instead of mod module; for file organization. When adding new modules, follow this pattern — add include!("new_module.rs"); to lib.rs.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple workers had to discover this pattern by reading lib.rs. glyph-cache-redesign worker specifically flagged this as orientation overhead in skillFeedback.

rejectedObservations.observation: No CLAUDE.md file in libraries/shaping/ violates canonical claim
rejectedObservations.reason: Pre-existing gap across ALL library directories (sys/, drawing/, ipc/, protocol/, scene/ also lack CLAUDE.md). Not introduced by this milestone and not actionable in the scrutiny validator scope. Should be addressed as a separate infrastructure task, not a feature review finding.

rejectedObservations.observation: Feature boundary overlap between build-system-cargo-deps and shaping-library (build-system worker implemented most of shaping-library's scope)
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a mission planning issue (feature scoping), not a code quality issue. The orchestrator already has this context from the workers' handoffs. Flagging it again provides no new information.

rejectedObservations.observation: CLAUDE.md formatting convention added in rasterizer-read-fonts-adaptation commit is unrelated scope creep
rejectedObservations.reason: The formatting convention is a positive contribution that documents existing project standards. It's not harmful scope creep — it's codebase documentation that was missing.
